intitule: Giving and Receiving Health Advice: Doctor-Patient Simulation

enonce.texte:
Lesson 7: Health and Prevention

Context: You are a medical intern at a community health center in Yaoundé. You have just examined a patient, Mr. Kamga, who has been practicing self-medication for a persistent fever and headache. Below is a list of common symptoms and medications available at the pharmacy.

Vocabulary Bank:

  • Symptoms: fever ($38.5^{\circ}C$), severe headache, fatigue, nausea
  • Medications: paracetamol (500mg), ibuprofen, antimalarial (Artemether-Lumefantrine)
  • Advice: prescription, dosage, hydration, rest, medical consultation

Grammar Focus: Modals for advice (should, shouldn't, must, have to)
images: A simple, clean illustration of a doctor in a white coat talking to a patient sitting on an examination table. The setting is a modest clinic in Cameroon. A small poster on the wall says 'No to Self-Medication' in English. The doctor holds a prescription pad.

questions.texte:
Analyze the patient's statement below. Identify the modal verbs of advice and explain their meaning in context.

Patient: "Doctor, I think I have malaria. I took some paracetamol I had at home, but my fever is still $39^{\circ}C$. You must tell me what to do. I shouldn't have taken that old medication, right?"
questions.reponse: The modal verbs are 'must' (expressing strong obligation/necessity) and 'shouldn't have' (expressing regret about a past action). 'Must tell' means the doctor is required to give instructions. 'Shouldn't have taken' means the patient regrets taking the old medication because it was a bad idea.

questions.texte: Complete the doctor's advice using the correct modal: 'should', 'shouldn't', 'must', or 'have to'.

sousQuestions.texte: You __________ (take) a blood test to confirm if it is malaria or a bacterial infection.
You __________ (buy) medication without a prescription. It is very dangerous.
You __________ (drink) at least 2 liters of water per day to stay hydrated.

questions.texte: Rewrite the following patient statements using the correct modal of advice to make them more polite and professional.

sousQuestions.texte:
Original: 'It is necessary for you to give me a prescription for a strong antibiotic.'
Rewritten: "Doctor, you __________ give me a prescription for a strong antibiotic."

Original: 'It is a good idea for the patient to rest for three days.'
Rewritten: "The patient __________ rest for three days."

intitule: Health Campaign: Writing Tips for a School Magazine

enonce.texte:
Context: Your school is organizing a health awareness week. As the editor of the school magazine 'The First Light', you have been asked to write a short article giving health tips to prevent the spread of respiratory diseases (like COVID-19 or the flu).

Data from the Ministry of Health:

  • 80% of respiratory infections are transmitted via hands.
  • Wearing a mask reduces transmission by 70% in crowded areas.
  • Self-medication with antibiotics does not work against viruses.
  • Vaccination reduces severe symptoms by 90%.

Grammar Focus: Conditional sentences (Type 1: If + present simple, ... will + infinitive)
images: A young student reading a colorful health brochure at a school desk in Cameroon. The brochure has a title 'Stop the Spread' with simple drawings of washing hands, wearing a mask, and a syringe (vaccine).

questions.texte: Formulate three conditional sentences (Type 1) using the data provided to explain the consequences of good or bad health practices.

sousQuestions.texte: Using the data about hand transmission.
sousQuestions.reponse: If you wash your hands regularly with soap, you will reduce the risk of transmitting respiratory infections.

sousQuestions.texte: Using the data about masks in crowded areas.
sousQuestions.reponse: If you wear a mask in crowded areas, you will protect yourself and others from the virus.
If you get vaccinated, you will have a much lower chance of developing severe symptoms.

questions.texte:
Create a short paragraph (4-5 sentences) for the school magazine. Your paragraph must:
- Include a title.
- Give three distinct health tips using 'should' or 'must'.
- Use at least one Type 1 conditional sentence.
- Warn against self-medication.
questions.reponse:
**Title: Protect Yourself, Protect Your Friends**

To stay healthy this flu season, you should wash your hands frequently with soap. You must avoid touching your eyes, nose, and mouth. If you feel sick with a fever or cough, you will need to wear a mask and see a nurse immediately. Remember, you shouldn't practice self-medication; antibiotics do not kill viruses. Finally, you must get vaccinated to protect our entire school community.

questions.texte:
Peer Review: Read the paragraph written by a classmate below. Identify and correct the two grammatical errors related to modals and conditionals.

Classmate's paragraph: "If you will have a high temperature, you must to stay at home. You should taking a lot of rest and drinking warm liquids. If you follow these rules, your health will improve quickly."
questions.reponse:
Error 1: 'If you will have a high temperature' is incorrect. Correct form: 'If you have a high temperature' (Type 1 conditional uses present simple in the 'if' clause).
Error 2: 'You must to stay at home' is incorrect. Correct form: 'You must stay at home' (Modal 'must' is followed by the base verb without 'to').
Error 3: 'You should taking' is incorrect. Correct form: 'You should take' (Modal 'should' is followed by the base verb).

Corrected paragraph: "If you have a high temperature, you must stay at home. You should take a lot of rest and drink warm liquids. If you follow these rules, your health will improve quickly."
